description:
You are a build-/release-engineer who has just received three freshly-built artifacts
    that need to be bundled and archived.  
    Your goal is to write a small shell-automation (roughly 5-10 commands) that does the
    following end-to-end workflow:

    1.  A shell script called
        /home/user/workspace/scripts/produce_release.sh must be created.
    2.  The script must accept one positional argument, the release **version string**
        (example: 1.0.0).  Do not prefix the version with “v”.
    3.  When the script is executed for the version 1.0.0 it shall:  
        a.  Collect the three files that already exist in
            /home/user/workspace/build_output/  
               • app_binary  
               • libhelper.so  
               • README.md  
        b.  Package them (and them only) into a gzip-compressed tarball named
            release_<VERSION>.tar.gz  
            e.g., release_1.0.0.tar.gz  
            The tarball’s internal directory layout must be flat – the three
            files must sit at the top of the archive (no sub-directory).  
        c.  Create a manifest file called checksums_<VERSION>.txt containing one line
            per file, each line having this exact pattern (two blank spaces after the
            hash):  
               <64-hex-digit-sha256>␠␠<filename>  
            The manifest must list the files in **alphabetical order**.  
        d.  Move both the archive and the checksum file into /home/user/releases/
            (create the directory if it does not yet exist).
        e.  Append one single-line entry to
            /home/user/releases/release_log.txt in the exact format below
            (pipe-delimited, no extra spaces):  
               <UNIX_EPOCH>|version=<VERSION>|files=app_binary,libhelper.so,README.md|checksum_manifest=checksums_<VERSION>.txt|archive=release_<VERSION>.tar.gz  
            Notes on the log line:  
               • <UNIX_EPOCH> is the current epoch-seconds timestamp  
               • The line must end with a newline (LF)  
               • Future runs of the script must always append, never overwrite.

    4.  After writing the script, execute it **once** for version 1.0.0 so that the
        release artifacts, checksum manifest and log line are all produced.
    5.  Finally, display the contents of the /home/user/releases directory so that the
        artefacts can be visually confirmed.

    The automated checker will verify:  
       • The script file exists and is executable.  
       • /home/user/releases contains release_1.0.0.tar.gz and
         checksums_1.0.0.txt.  
       • checksums_1.0.0.txt has exactly three lines, each following the pattern  
           ^[a-f0-9]{64}  (app_binary|libhelper\.so|README\.md)$  
         and listed alphabetically.  
       • release_1.0.0.tar.gz, when listed with “tar -tzf”, shows exactly  
           app_binary  
           libhelper.so  
           README.md  
       • The **last** line of /home/user/releases/release_log.txt matches  
           ^[0-9]{10}\|version=1\.0\.0\|files=app_binary,libhelper\.so,README\.md\|checksum_manifest=checksums_1\.0\.0\.txt\|archive=release_1\.0\.0\.tar\.gz$  

    You may solve the task any way you like, but stay within ordinary user
    privileges (no sudo) and try to keep the command count modest (about 5-10
    commands is perfectly fine).
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing environment BEFORE the agent starts
    -->
    1.  Directory /home/user/workspace/build_output/ already exists.
        It contains three plain-text files:

        /home/user/workspace/build_output/app_binary
            CONTENTS (no trailing newline):
               HelloAppBinaryV100

        /home/user/workspace/build_output/libhelper.so
            CONTENTS (no trailing newline):
               SharedObjectV100

        /home/user/workspace/build_output/README.md
            CONTENTS (no trailing newline):
               Build artifacts for release 1.0.0

    2.  Directory /home/user/workspace/scripts/ exists and is writable by the user,
        but is initially empty.

    3.  Directory /home/user/releases/ does NOT exist before the task starts.

    4.  Expected artefacts AFTER the correct execution of the agent’s script
        (for version 1.0.0):

        • /home/user/workspace/scripts/produce_release.sh
              – must be executable (chmod 755 or similar).

        • /home/user/releases/release_1.0.0.tar.gz
              – a gzipped tar containing exactly:
                    ./app_binary
                    ./libhelper.so
                    ./README.md
              – tarball must be reproducible by “tar -tzf” to show these three
                entries with no directory prefix beyond ./.

        • /home/user/releases/checksums_1.0.0.txt
              – exactly three lines, LF-terminated, alphabetical order:
                    SHA256(app_binary)␠␠app_binary
                    SHA256(libhelper.so)␠␠libhelper.so
                    SHA256(README.md)␠␠README.md
              – where SHA256(file) is the hash of the file content above.
                (The tester will compute and compare.)

        • /home/user/releases/release_log.txt
              – final line conforms to regex:
                    ^[0-9]{10}\|version=1\.0\.0\|files=app_binary,libhelper\.so,README\.md\|checksum_manifest=checksums_1\.0\.0\.txt\|archive=release_1\.0\.0\.tar\.gz$
              – There may be earlier lines if the agent decides to test-run
                multiple versions; the tester only inspects the last line.

    5.  Permissions:
        All artefacts must be owned by the unprivileged user and be readable
        (644) while scripts should be executable (755).

    6.  The evaluator will run the following commands (conceptually) to grade:
         • test -x /home/user/workspace/scripts/produce_release.sh
         • ls /home/user/releases | grep -E 'release_1\.0\.0\.tar\.gz'
         • ls /home/user/releases | grep -E 'checksums_1\.0\.0\.txt'
         • grep -E '^[0-9]{10}\|version=1\.0\.0\|files=app_binary,libhelper\.so,README\.md\|checksum_manifest=checksums_1\.0\.0\.txt\|archive=release_1\.0\.0\.tar\.gz$' /home/user/releases/release_log.txt
         • tar -tzf /home/user/releases/release_1.0.0.tar.gz | sort
            (expects output: app_binary  libhelper.so  README.md)
         • awk '{print $2}' /home/user/releases/checksums_1.0.0.txt | sort
            (expects same three filenames)
         • sha256sum -c /home/user/releases/checksums_1.0.0.txt
            (must report OK for all three files)
